Description: A unique identifier for each synthetic object.
A flag indicating the pulsar type (e.g., Normal, MSP, RRAT).
The true age of the pulsar since its formation (in years).
The final, evolved spin period of the pulsar (in seconds).
The final, evolved rate of change of the spin period (in s/s).
The final, evolved surface magnetic field strength (in Gauss).
The birth spin period of the pulsar (in seconds).
The birth magnetic field strength (in Gauss).
The final, evolved position of the pulsar in the Galaxy (in degrees).
The final, evolved position of the pulsar in the Galaxy (in degrees).
The final, evolved distance from the Sun (in kpc).
The integrated column density of free electrons along the line of sight (in pc cm⁻³).
The apparent motion on the sky due to the pulsar's space velocity (in mas/yr).
The magnitude of the natal kick imparted at birth (in km/s).
A boolean flag (True/False) indicating if the pulsar is in a binary system.
The period of the binary orbit (in days).
The mass of the binary companion (in solar masses).
The eccentricity of the binary orbit.
The intrinsic radio luminosity at 1400 MHz (in mJy kpc²).
The distribution model for single pulses (e.g., 'Log-Normal' or 'Exponential'), particularly for RRATs.
The fraction of the sky covered by the pulsar's radio beam.
The angle between the observer's line-of-sight and the pulsar's rotation axis.
The angle between the pulsar's magnetic and rotation axes.
The fraction of time the pulsar's emission is off (0 for persistent, >0.9 for RRATs).
